_comment: Persistent-object factory. Every object is a PixelLab 8-direction object (create-8-direction-object) sized for its type (32-256), Grave Seasons style, scaled vs a 64px character. ANIMATIONS BY OBVIOUSNESS: default 0; add 1 if the object has one obvious motion (fire flicker, foliage sway in wind, a chest opening, a creature moving), 2 if it clearly has two (a campfire: flames + smoke). Animations use max frames (16) across all 8 directions.
style_base: cozy painterly RPG pixel art in the exact style of Grave Seasons, a cohesive muted-but-warm seasonal palette, soft naturalistic lighting with strong ambient occlusion and gently rounded volumes, subtle selective darker-tone outlines (NOT a hard uniform black cartoon outline), rich high detail, crisp deliberate pixels, a single centered game prop on a transparent background
_style_version: Bump this when the look changes. Objects whose manifest records an older style_version are considered stale; run the loop with --restyle (or the workflow's restyle input) to delete and regenerate them in the new style.

defaults.negative_description: blurry, multiple objects, duplicated, cropped, text, watermark, drop shadow, background scenery, tiling, thick uniform black cartoon outline, sticker look, flat solid colors, low effort

scale._comment: REALISM RULE: objects are scaled to the world by real height, so they sit correctly next to characters. A character is ~character_height_px tall (the 64px skeleton) representing character_height_m. The loop turns each object's world_height_m into world_px_height = round(world_height_m * character_height_px / character_height_m); a game renders the sprite scaled to that pixel height. So a coin (~0.22m) is tiny next to a character and an oak (~6m) towers. Every object MUST have a world_height_m (per-object, else category_height_m fallback).

budget._comment: Shared PixelLab pool floor (see coordination/PROTOCOL.md). The loop stops cleanly when the balance drops below this, leaving headroom for the characters and maps domains that draw from the same pool.

targets._comment: How many objects the loop aims to produce. It builds the explicit `catalog` first, then synthesizes further ones from `procedural` until this count is reached. Raise it to keep the loop generating open-endedly.

_procedural: When the explicit catalog is exhausted but targets.num_objects is not yet reached, the loop synthesizes more objects by combining these axes. Deterministic (seeded by index) so re-runs are reproducible. These are best-effort, un-curated extras.

animations.description: the leaves swaying in the wind

_animations: Every object is a persistent 8-direction PixelLab object (create-8-direction-object) with EXACTLY 3 animations chosen to fit it, each animated across all 8 directions.
